top_requirements.requirement: Cloud platform depth (AWS most common, then Azure, then GCP) named as a core qual
top_requirements.approx_frequency: AWS in 61/103, Azure in 39/103, GCP in 20/103 postings

top_requirements.requirement: Cloud/platform certifications listed as required or preferred (AWS Solutions Architect, Azure Architect, CISSP, platform-specific certs like Anaplan/Adobe)
Client/stakeholder-facing communication and executive presence (workshops, steering committees, C-level conversations)
DevOps/CI-CD and Infrastructure-as-Code fluency (Terraform named most often)
top_requirements.approx_frequency: DevOps in 49/103, CI/CD in 40/103, Terraform in 33/103
'professional services' in 43/103 (concentrated in Caylent, Anaplan, Klaviyo, Contentful, New Relic, Chainguard, Snowflake)

top_requirements.requirement: Hands-on coding/scripting (Python most common, then Java, SQL)
top_requirements.approx_frequency: Python in 29/103, Java in 20/103, SQL in 18/103

top_requirements.requirement: Pre-sales / proposal / SOW / level-of-effort estimation experience
Willingness to travel to client/site locations
top_requirements.approx_frequency: 'travel' appears in 49/103 (ranges from 15-25% at Chainguard/Caylent to on-site/hybrid requirements at GDIT)

top_requirements.requirement: Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience, plus a stated years-of-experience floor (commonly 5-10+ years, sometimes 10+ for principal/SME titles)
Active US government security clearance (Secret/TS, US citizenship required) — concentrated entirely in the federal-SI subset of the sample
top_requirements.approx_frequency: 'clearance' in 21/103, effectively all from GDIT's 19 postings

top_responsibilities: Lead discovery and scoping workshops with customer/client stakeholders, translating business requirements into a technical solution design, level-of-effort estimate, and Statement of Work or proposal — this pre-sales-to-SOW handoff pattern recurs across SaaS professional-services orgs (Klaviyo, Caylent, Anaplan, Chainguard, Contentful) and consulting-style SI postings alike.

Own end-to-end delivery of an implementation engagement as the senior technical owner — driving the project to on-time/on-budget completion, coordinating with engagement/project managers, and remaining accountable through go-live (Effectual, Trace3, Anaplan, New Relic, Klaviyo).

Act as trusted technical advisor and executive-level interface — presenting architecture and strategy directly to CTOs/CIOs/VP-level stakeholders and steering committees, distinct from a purely internal engineering role (Anaplan, Thoughtworks, Effectual, Trace3).

Produce and document cloud/data/integration architecture artifacts: reference architectures, landing zones, blueprints, sequence/integration diagrams, and Infrastructure-as-Code standards (Effectual, Robots & Pencils, GDIT, DEPT, Solita, VML).

Support the pre-sales motion specifically — qualifying opportunities, scoping with prospects, authoring commercial proposals, and partnering with (not reporting to) Account Executives; several postings explicitly separate this pre-sales SA function from the delivery-side architect function within the same company (clearest example: Caylent runs distinct 'Customer Solutions Architect' [pre-sales] vs. 'Cloud/Cloud Software Architect' [delivery/build] job families).

Mentor and enable internal delivery engineers, client-side engineering teams, and (for partner-facing roles) reseller/SI partner teams — architecture reviews, technical playbooks, and knowledge-transfer sessions (Effectual, Chainguard, Robots & Pencils).

Configure/build within a specific enterprise platform's own modeling or design environment (Anaplan multi-dimensional models, Adobe AEP/AEM, Salesforce Commerce Cloud, SAP), translating business logic directly into platform-specific build artifacts rather than general-purpose software engineering (Anaplan, VML, DEPT, Reply).

(Federal-SI subset only, ~19 of 103 postings, all GDIT) Hands-on cloud migration/modernization engineering for a single government program under FedRAMP/DISA-STIG/NIST compliance — architecting and often personally implementing GovCloud environments, IaC, and security controls, with no pre-sales, RFP, or multi-client portfolio language present.

comp_signals.range_text: Senior Partner Solutions Architect (East, Professional Services): "up to $195,000 base salary + bonus + equity"
ICAM Solutions Architect SME: likely salary range $255,000 - $345,000
Cloud Architect / Developer SME: likely salary range $187,179 - $230,000
Principal AI/ML Architect (USA): base salary range $165,000 - $205,000
Senior Customer Solution Architect (USA): base salary range $195,000 - $216,000
Sr. Solutions Architect | Security (Northern California): estimated pay range $180,000 - $215,000 (base only, explicitly excludes bonus/commission)
Liferay Technical Architect: offered salary range $120,000 - $180,000
SAP Cloud Architect: base compensation range $100,000 - $180,000

Associate Solution Architect, Professional Services (NY): OTE (includes base+commission) $102,000 - $138,000 — notable because this is explicitly commission-based OTE, not a flat base+modest-bonus structure
Principal Solution Architect, Professional Services - Finance (NYC): base salary range $171,000 - $232,000
Senior Solution Architect, Professional Services - Workforce Planning (Miami): base salary range $147,000 - $198,000

seniority_distribution_notes: Sample skews senior-to-principal: 23 titles contain "Senior"/"Sr.", 20 contain "Principal", only 2 contain "Associate," and none contain "Manager" or "Lead" as the primary title word (management-track titles largely absent from this corpus). No junior/entry-level "Solutions Architect" titles appear at all — consistent with the brief's framing of SA as a mid-to-senior lateral/promotion destination rather than an entry point. However, the sample is heavily concentrated in three companies (Caylent 33, GDIT 19, Anaplan 10 — 61 of 103 rows, ~59%), so this seniority skew partly reflects those three employers' hiring patterns rather than a fully independent cross-market read.
notable_divergence_from_existing_brief: Two notable divergences. (1) The brief frames this archetype almost entirely around large SI/consultancies (Accenture, Deloitte, Slalom, Capgemini) with utilization-linked, non-commission bonus comp. This fresh sample skews toward a different population: SaaS-vendor Professional Services orgs (Anaplan, Klaviyo, Contentful, New Relic, Snowflake, Chainguard) and AWS-reseller/SI shops (Caylent), none of which are in the brief's named-company list, plus a large federal-SI block (GDIT, 19/103 postings) that the brief never discusses. GDIT postings read materially differently from the brief's "portfolio of concurrent client engagements, RFP/pre-sales, utilization metric" picture: no RFP, pre-sales, proposal, SOW, or utilization language appears in any GDIT posting; instead they describe hands-on cloud migration/engineering embedded on one government program under FedRAMP/clearance requirements, which is closer to a Consulting Engineer / hands-on delivery archetype than to the brief's design-and-handoff SA. This is worth flagging: the "Solutions Architect" title on public ATS boards pulls in a federal-contractor engineering population the brief's sourcing (built from Accenture/Deloitte-style postings) didn't capture. (2) The Contentful posting's OTE explicitly includes commission ("OTE (includes base+commission): $102,000-$138,000"), directly contradicting the brief's central claim (Section 3, repeated in the taxonomy's variable_comp_appetite rationale) that consulting-SA bonus is utilization/margin-linked and structurally distinct from sales commission. This is a single data point, but it is a direct company-stated commission structure, stronger evidence than the brief's own inferred utilization-bonus link — worth a closer look before treating "not commission-driven" as settled for this archetype. On the positive side, most other comp figures found (Chainguard, Trace3, Effectual, GDIT, Caylent, Anaplan) show base+modest-bonus with no commission language, so the brief's claim holds for the majority but not universally.

dimension_evidence.note: Postings consistently show discovery/scoping work that converges on a defined SOW or proposal deliverable (Klaviyo, Caylent, Anaplan), matching a mid-level ambiguity target rather than fully open-ended research.

Multiple concurrent engagements/workshops language appears (Anaplan, Effectual 'across programs', Trace3 'regional' scope) though no posting quantifies concurrent-engagement count directly, so this remains inferred rather than directly stated.

No paging/on-call/incident-SLA language found in the sample except one brief GDIT-adjacent mention of 'incident response' training/workshops at Effectual, which is enablement not a personal on-call duty — supports the low target.

Sample shows architecture review and troubleshooting language (Effectual: 'Troubleshoot complex infrastructure challenges') but not live production on-call debugging under an SLA clock for most postings — GDIT subset is the exception (see divergence note) and may deserve a higher figure if federal-SI is folded into this archetype.
Strongly supported: blueprints, landing zones, multi-account/multi-cloud designs, reference architectures appear across Effectual, Robots & Pencils, GDIT, DEPT.

Well supported by raw frequency: AWS/Azure/GCP/Kubernetes/Terraform/multiple platform certs all appear widely, and several postings (Robots & Pencils, Effectual) explicitly span cloud+data+AI/ML in one role — consistent with a high breadth target.

Mixed signal: several postings (Effectual, Klaviyo, Anaplan, New Relic) explicitly hold the SA accountable for delivery being 'on time, on budget, and in line with agreed outcomes' or 'measured by ... long-term strategic adoption' — stronger production/outcome ownership language than the brief's 'design that gets handed off' framing implies. Worth reviewing whether 2 is too low for the vendor-PS-heavy subset of this sample, even though it likely still fits the pure pre-sales SA sub-role (e.g., Caylent's Customer Solutions Architect, which explicitly says 'This is not a delivery role').
Extremely well supported — 'stakeholder' appears in 74/103 and 'client' in 67/103 postings; executive-level interface language (CTO/CIO) recurs across Anaplan, Thoughtworks, Effectual.
Mentorship/enablement language present but scoped to one client's team or internal engineers/partners (Effectual, Chainguard, Robots & Pencils), not public teaching — consistent with a modest target.

Sample doesn't strongly confirm a 'wide portfolio' pattern for the SaaS-vendor-PS subset — several postings (Klaviyo, Anaplan Principal SA) instead describe owning a small number of large strategic-account engagements end-to-end ('own strategic customer engagements end-to-end'), which reads closer to deep/narrow account focus than a wide utilization-driven portfolio. The federal-SI subset (GDIT) is the opposite extreme — typically one program, one client, long-term embed. Recommend re-checking whether 5 (very wide breadth) still fits once this more vendor-PS-heavy sample is weighed against the original Accenture/Deloitte-anchored evidence.

Some postings support low continuity (SOW-bounded engagements, Caylent's pre-sales-to-implementation handoff), but others explicitly emphasize long-term adoption and multi-year relationship ownership (Klaviyo: 'long-term adoption'; New Relic: 'long-term strategic adoption of the platform') — this sample shows more relationship-continuity language than the current low target implies, at least for the vendor-PS subset.

Mostly supported (Chainguard, Trace3, Effectual, GDIT, Caylent, Anaplan all show base+modest-bonus, no-commission structures matching the brief), BUT the Contentful posting explicitly states an OTE including commission ($102K-$138K OTE, base+commission) — a direct company-stated exception. Given this is a high-weight (0.9) dimension, this single counter-example is worth flagging for review even though it doesn't overturn the majority pattern.

Bimodal: pre-sales-flavored postings (Caylent Customer Solutions Architect, Klaviyo, Anaplan PS) match the low-coding target well, but several delivery-side postings in this sample (Effectual: 'Develop Infrastructure-as-Code using Terraform... Create Python automation scripts'; Robots & Pencils: hands-on multi-domain build; Caylent Cloud Software Architect: 'delegate tickets to a team of engineers') describe meaningfully more hands-on build work than 'codes mainly to prototype.' Recommend the reviewer check whether this archetype should be split or the target nudged up for the delivery-heavy sub-population this new sample surfaced.

Firm-dependent pattern confirmed: Chainguard/Caylent state 15-25% travel, while GDIT roles are largely on-site/hybrid at a single government facility — consistent with a mid-target, firm-dependent framing.

GDIT subset (19/103 postings) shows meaningfully more security-control language than the brief anticipated — NIST-aligned controls, DISA STIGs/CIS benchmarks, FedRAMP compliance, RBAC/least-privilege design are named responsibilities, not incidental mentions. If federal-SI postings are considered in-scope for this archetype, 1 may be too low for that subset specifically, though it likely still fits the non-federal majority.
No hardware/power/timing constraint language found anywhere in the 103-posting sample — supports the low target.

Sample shows mentorship of engineers/partners as a responsibility (Effectual, Chainguard) but no formal people-management/headcount-owner titles in the corpus (0 'Manager'/'Lead' primary titles) — consistent with a low-to-modest target.
No conference-speaking, blog, or public-content requirements found in the sample; all client-facing work described is account-specific and private — supports the low target.

This sample shows more AI/ML-specific SA roles than a target of 1 implies — Snowflake's posting is titled 'Senior Solutions Architect - AI/ML', Caylent runs a distinct 'Principal AI/ML Architect' and 'Machine Learning Architect' job family (10 postings combined), and Robots & Pencils explicitly requires MLOps/LLM/feature-store experience. This looks like a real, non-trivial sub-population within the archetype that the current target treats as irrelevant; worth a closer look, though it may reflect 2026 market drift (AI-infra hiring surge) rather than the archetype's timeless core.
No mobile-platform requirements found anywhere in the sample — supports the low target.

Data architecture (warehouses, pipelines, Snowflake/Redshift/BigQuery) is a named, recurring responsibility across several postings (Robots & Pencils, Solita's dedicated 'Data Architect' openings, VML's Snowflake & AI role, Snowflake's own SA posting) — a target of 1 (not relevant) seems understated given how often data-platform work appears as core scope, not incidental, in this sample.

Given that AWS/Azure/GCP appear in the majority of postings and several roles (Effectual, GDIT, Caylent's Cloud Architect family) describe hands-on building of landing zones, IaC, and multi-account architectures — not just advisory-level breadth — a target of 2 (low weight, design-only) looks understated for a meaningful chunk of this sample, though it may still fit the pure pre-sales/advisory sub-role well.
